setting.label: Profile Preset
setting.values: Auto Max (Best Overall)|Audiophile (Prioritize Audio)|Resolution (Prioritize 4K)|Remux (Prioritize Size/Bitrate)|1080p Focus (Standard HD)|Light Mode (Slow Connection / Low-End Device)
setting.help: Auto Max: balanced best-of-everything. Audiophile: Atmos/TrueHD first. Resolution: 4K above all. Remux: biggest file wins. 1080p Focus: penalises 4K. Light Mode: smallest/lowest-bitrate stream wins — ideal for slow connections or older hardware.

setting.label: Server Tie-Breaker Preference
setting.help: If both servers score identically, which server wins automatically?

setting.label: Single Server Mode
setting.help: Enable if you only have one server (Emby OR Jellyfin). Disables head-to-head comparison, tie dialogs and the manual picker.

setting.label: Manually Select Winning Results
setting.help: A selection dialog always appears after scoring so you can choose which server to play from. Has no effect when Single Server Mode is on.

setting.label: Deep Dive Searching
setting.help: When ON, the engine casts a massively wider net — more API calls, relaxed name matching, lower confidence thresholds — so virtually nothing on your servers is skipped. Results still go through the full confidence funnel and scoring pipeline. Slower but catches edge-case matches that normal mode would miss. Best left OFF unless you notice missing content.

setting.label: TMDB API Key (optional)
setting.help: Your own TMDB v3 API key. If blank, Source Engine borrows TMDb Helper's key automatically.

setting.label: Auto-fill from EmbyCon
setting.help: Reads your server address, username and password from EmbyCon and fills them in automatically.

setting.label: Server Address
setting.help: The direct web address of your Emby server (e.g., https://yourdomain.com).

setting.label: Username
setting.help: Your Emby account username.

setting.label: Password
setting.help: Your Emby account password. Used to obtain an internal API token on Kodi launch.

setting.label: Test Connection
setting.help: Pings your Emby server with the stored token. Shows a progress bar while connecting, then a result notification.

setting.label: Auto-fill from EmbyCon
setting.help: Configure EmbyCon to point at your backup Emby server first, then press this to import those settings here.

setting.label: Backup Server Address
setting.help: Full URL of your backup Emby server (e.g., https://emby-backup.example.com). Leave blank to disable failover.

setting.label: Backup Username
setting.help: Your backup Emby account username.

setting.label: Backup Password
setting.help: Password for your backup Emby account.

setting.label: Test Backup Connection
setting.help: Pings the backup Emby server with its stored token.

setting.label: Prefer Backup Server
setting.help: When enabled, always uses the backup Emby server instead of the primary, even if the primary is reachable.

setting.label: Auto-fill from JellyCon
setting.help: Reads your server address and username from JellyCon and fills them in automatically.

setting.label: Server Address
setting.help: The direct web address of your Jellyfin server (e.g., https://yourdomain.com).

setting.label: Username
setting.help: Your Jellyfin account username.

setting.label: Password
setting.help: Your Jellyfin account password. Used to obtain an internal API token on Kodi launch.

setting.label: Test Connection
setting.help: Pings your Jellyfin server with the stored token. Shows a progress bar while connecting, then a result notification.

setting.label: Auto-fill from JellyCon
setting.help: Configure JellyCon to point at your backup Jellyfin server first, then press this to import those settings here.

setting.label: Backup Server Address
setting.help: Full URL of your backup Jellyfin server (e.g., https://jellyfin-backup.example.com). Leave blank to disable failover.

setting.label: Backup Username
setting.help: Your backup Jellyfin account username.

setting.label: Backup Password
setting.help: Password for your backup Jellyfin account. Note: JellyCon does not store passwords — only Source Engine Pro uses this for auto-login.

setting.label: Test Backup Connection
setting.help: Pings the backup Jellyfin server with its stored token.

setting.label: Prefer Backup Server
setting.help: When enabled, always uses the backup Jellyfin server instead of the primary, even if the primary is reachable.

setting.label: Audio Scoring Mode
setting.help: Preset Controlled: audio bonuses are handled automatically by your chosen Profile Preset. Custom Scoring: you define exact score bonuses for each audio format — overrides the preset's audio logic entirely.

setting.label: Atmos Score Bonus
setting.help: Score bonus added when a stream has Dolby Atmos. Higher = more likely to win. Only used in Custom Scoring mode. Default: 300.

setting.label: DTS:X Score Bonus
setting.help: Score bonus added when a stream has DTS:X spatial audio. Default: 250.

setting.label: TrueHD Score Bonus
setting.help: Score bonus added when a stream has Dolby TrueHD lossless audio. Default: 200.

setting.label: DTS-HD MA Score Bonus
setting.help: Score bonus added when a stream has DTS-HD Master Audio lossless. Default: 175.

setting.label: FLAC / PCM Score Bonus
setting.help: Score bonus added when a stream has FLAC or uncompressed PCM audio. Default: 100.

setting.label: Surround 5.1+ Score Bonus
setting.help: Score bonus added when a stream has 5 or more audio channels. Stacks on top of codec bonuses. Default: 50.

setting.label: Stereo Penalty (subtracted)
setting.help: Score points subtracted when a stream has only 2 channels (stereo or mono). Useful if you always want surround. Default: 0 (no penalty).

setting.label: Audio Requirement Filter
setting.values: No Requirement|Require Spatial (Atmos / DTS:X)|Require Lossless|Require Surround (5.1+)
setting.help: Hard filter: streams that don't meet this audio standard are buried (score set to -9999) and will only play if nothing else qualifies. Only active in Custom Scoring mode.

setting.label: Max Resolution Limit
setting.help: Prevents picking files above this resolution. Great for slow internet or older TVs.

setting.label: Max File Size (GB) [0 = No Limit]
setting.help: Filters out files larger than this size in GB. Leave 0 for no limit.

setting.label: Text Color Theme
setting.help: Changes the color theme of the notification text.

setting.label: Startup Health Notifications
setting.help: Show a toast on Kodi startup confirming server connection status (OK / FAIL). Disable if you don't want any pop-ups at boot.

setting.label: Show Server Stats on Startup
setting.help: When enabled, Source Engine Pro displays a toast with live server stats (latency, streams, library size) after each Kodi boot. Stats appear after the normal health-check notification. Requires Startup Health Notifications to be enabled.

setting.label: Ping Status Notifications
setting.help: Show a periodic toast comparing server latency. Fires on startup and every hour while Kodi is running. Disable to keep things quiet.

setting.label: Failover / Backup Notifications
setting.help: Show toasts when Source Engine Pro switches to a backup server (primary down) or restores the primary connection. Disable to suppress failover pop-ups.
